title: Elder Law - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Elder Law 10 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES INC CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides civil legal aid and advocacy, can not assist with criminal law cases. Organized into specialty units: Housing/Consumer Unit, Income Maintenance Unit, Employment/Individual Rights Unit (Immigration Law Project, Protecting Our Veterans, and Public Guardian Program), and Family Unit. 225 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Low-income households (125% of the Federal Poverty Level), age 60 and older, and veterans. Specialty units may have additional eligibility requirements. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm (541) 779-7292 Get Directions More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW SOUTHEAST MULTICULTURAL SENIOR CENTER SENIOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults such as information and assistance/referral, Senior Law Project, and AARP tax preparation assistance. 4610 SE Belmont Street Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and caregivers for older adults Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 721-6760 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON DOUGLAS COUNTY OFFICE SENIOR LAW PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice, referral, and representation to older adults on a range of legal issues, such as assisted living facility problems, guardianship and conservatorship defense, debt collection, and financial abuse. Eligibility: Age 60 and older and their spouses. Must reside in or have a legal issue in Douglas County. Must be a US citizen or lawful resident. Hours: In-person applications: Monday-Wednesday 10:30am-noon Phone intake hours vary depending on available staff (541) 673-1181 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE SENIOR LAW PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice, referral, and representation to older adults on a range of legal issues, such as assisted living facility problems, guardianship and conservatorship defense, debt collection, and financial abuse. Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-11:50am and 1pm-3:50pm (503) 224-4086 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON SALEM REGIONAL OFFICE SENIOR LAW PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal advice, referral, and representation to older adults on a range of legal issues, such as assisted living facility problems, guardianship and conservatorship defense, debt collection, and financial abuse. Eligibility: Age 60 and older. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 2pm-5pm, Friday 9am-noon (503) 581-5265 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER COOS BAY REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 490 N 2nd Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Walk in: Monday/Wednesday 1:30pm-4pm Telephone hours only: Tuesday/Thursday 1:30pm-4pm (541) 269-1226 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER GRANTS PASS REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Offers a senior law hotline. Some locations may offer additional services. 424 NW 6th Street Suite 102 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 1pm-4pm (541) 471-3033 1-4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER LANE COUNTY LEGAL AID GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides general legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 101 E Broadway Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-11:45am and 12:45pm-5pm Intake for new clients: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm-3pm (541) 485-1017 100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER LANE COUNTY LEGAL AID SENIOR LAW SERVICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential legal services for civil (non-criminal) issues only. Group lectures and discussions are offered. Services are provided by staff and volunteer attorneys. Self-help pamphlets and brochures available. 101 E Broadway Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 60 and older Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Appointment hours: -Saturday mornings and some Tuesday/Wednesday appointments at 2 senior centers: Campbell Community Center (155 High Street, Eugene, OR 97401) and Willamalane Adult Activity Center (215 W C Street, Springfield, OR 97477) -1st Friday of the month at Cottage Grove Senior Center (700 E Gibbs Avenue, Cottage Grove, OR 97424) -Florence residents, call Program Director for volunteer attorney hours (541) 485-1017 100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website offers legal information regarding various issues, including housing, family law, employment law, elder law, government benefits, and consumer protection. Provides links to legal service providers, resources, and a directory of legal aid providers. 621 SW Morrison Street Suite 1450 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (503) 224-4086 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects older adults with services that allow them to remain safely in their homes and communities (elder law, home maintenance and minor repair services, housekeeping assistance, Medicare counseling, housing information and referral, advocacy, and senior ride program). Services include Oregon Project Independence Services (OPI) and community resources. Additionally offers short-term case management (Options Counseling) and chronic disease management. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 60 and older with a focus on the African-American community Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES INC CENTER FOR NONPROFIT LEGAL SERVICES IMPACT NW SENIOR SERVICES OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND SENIOR INFORMATION AND ASSISTANCE
